name: Garden onion (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw bulb, Allium cepa)

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Free glucose is the single most abundant free sugar in raw onion; primary readily-fermentable carbon source.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Second free sugar; also the monomer released by hydrolysis of onion fructans.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Disaccharide, glucose+fructose.

components.name: fructans / fructo-oligosaccharides (inulin-type)
components.source: Jansen et al. 2024, J Food Compost Anal PMC11327153 (onion 1.97 g/100g); PMC8624415 (red onion 3.7 g/100g)
components.evidence: Onion is a major dietary fructan source; kestose/nystose/inulin GF(n) chains are a large fermentable carbon reservoir beyond the free sugars. Value is a conservative midpoint (range ~2-5 g/100g, cultivar/storage dependent).

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Rodriguez Galdon et al. 2008 JAFC doi:10.1021/jf800282h (43.6 mg/100g); Liguori et al. 2017 J Food Quality doi:10.1155/2017/6873651 (57.6-78.9 mg/100g)
components.evidence: One of the two dominant free organic acids of onion flesh; midpoint of reported means (range 44-79).

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Rodriguez Galdon et al. 2008 JAFC doi:10.1021/jf800282h (48.5 mg/100g); Liguori et al. 2017 (19.7-63.3 mg/100g)
components.evidence: Co-dominant free organic acid with malic; MISSING from the FooDB profile.

components.name: pyruvic acid
components.source: Rodriguez Galdon et al. 2008 JAFC doi:10.1021/jf800282h; Yoo et al. tandfonline doi:10.1080/10942912.2015.1042589
components.evidence: Enzymatically released pungency marker of onion (alliinase product); genuine and growth-relevant. MISSING from FooDB profile. Value is a mild-cultivar background level (range widens to ~90 mg/100g in pungent bulbs).

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Rodriguez Galdon et al. 2008 JAFC doi:10.1021/jf800282h (113 mg/kg)
components.evidence: Minor free organic acid.

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Liguori et al. 2017 J Food Quality doi:10.1155/2017/6873651
components.evidence: Reported as a principal cyclitol/organic acid of onion but not cleanly quantified in fresh-weight terms; included qualitatively.

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Rodriguez Galdon et al. 2008 JAFC doi:10.1021/jf800282h
components.evidence: Present in the 7-acid onion HPLC profile; minor, not individually quantified here.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: Rodriguez Galdon et al. 2008 JAFC doi:10.1021/jf800282h
components.evidence: Trace organic acid detected in onion profile.

components.name: tartaric acid
components.evidence: Trace, reported qualitatively in landrace onion profile.

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Most abundant protein-derived amino acid; also a major free amino acid in onion.

components.name: L-glutamate (glutamic acid)
components.source: USDA FDC 170000; free-acid form 325 mg/100g in Rodriguez Galdon 2008 doi:10.1021/jf800282h
components.evidence: Dominant amino acid; free glutamate is the single most abundant free acid measured in onion (umami, and a growth N/C source).

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low protein food; amino acids above are the protein-hydrolysate profile.

components.name: S-alk(en)yl-L-cysteine sulfoxides (isoalliin, methiin, propiin)
components.source: Rose et al. 2005 Nat Prod Rep; onion flavor-precursor literature
components.evidence: Defining onion metabolites and a substantial organic-sulfur/carbon reservoir (~0.2-1% FW as flavor precursors); alliinase products (thiosulfinates, pyruvate, ammonia). Present but not quantified in FDC/FooDB; growth-relevant S source, included qualitatively.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: Food folate = DFE (no folic acid fortification).

components.name: vitamin B12
components.evidence: Absent (plant food); B12 supplementation required for cobalamin-auxotroph growth.

components.name: beta-carotene (provitamin A)
components.evidence: Vitamin A RAE effectively 0.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation.

components.name: sulfur
components.evidence: Onion is a sulfur-accumulator; total S (sulfate + cysteine-sulfoxides) is a defining, growth-relevant macro-element. Not quantified in FDC.

components.name: molybdenum (molybdate)
components.evidence: Genuine trace micronutrient (nitrate-reductase/molybdoenzyme cofactor); present but unquantified.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Onion is essentially non-fatty (0.1 g total fat/100g); fatty acids negligible for growth.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1)
components.evidence: Negligible; retained for completeness.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Negligible.

components.name: dietary fiber (insoluble cell wall)
components.evidence: Largely non-fructan structural fiber (fructans reported separately above).

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid soil contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: animal muscle metabolite, biologically implausible in a plant bulb; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: animal metabolite, implausible in onion; FooDB predicted

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: synthetic human contraceptive steroid; implausible/predicted, not a food nutrient

remove.name: Prostaglandin A-1
remove.reason: animal eicosanoid, biologically implausible in a plant; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Diphenylamine
remove.reason: synthetic post-harvest anti-scald pesticide/contaminant, not a native nutrient

remove.name: Fluorine
remove.reason: trace halogen (1.1 µg/100g), not a growth nutrient

missing_added: citric acid (48.5 mg/100g) - absent from FooDB profile, co-dominant organic acid
malic acid (~55 mg/100g) - co-dominant organic acid
oxalic acid, succinic, fumaric, tartaric, quinic acids
fructans / fructo-oligosaccharides (~2 g/100g) - major fermentable carbon reservoir
quantitative free sugars split (glucose 1.97 > fructose 1.29 > sucrose 0.99 g/100g)
keep_uncovered: Alliospiroside A/B/C/D, Alliofuroside A - onion steroidal saponins
Caffeic acid, trans-Sinapic acid, ferulate, gallic acid, vanillate - phenolic acids
L-DOPA, Dopamine - trace catecholamines genuine to onion
Boron - genuine plant micronutrient (no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170000 (Onions, raw, SR Legacy)
Rodriguez Galdon, Rodriguez Rodriguez & Diaz Romero 2008, J Agric Food Chem 56(15):6512 doi:10.1021/jf800282h (organic acids in onion cultivars)
Liguori et al. 2017, J Food Quality 2017:6873651 doi:10.1155/2017/6873651 (organic acids, white onion landraces)
Yoo et al. 2015, Int J Food Prop doi:10.1080/10942912.2015.1042589 (validated HPLC pyruvic acid in onion)
Jansen et al. 2024, fructan content Slovenian foods, PMC11327153
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for growth are the free hexoses (glucose 1.97 > fructose 1.29 g/100g) plus a large inulin-type fructan reservoir (~2 g/100g) that hydrolyses to fructose+glucose. Organic acids (malic + citric ~50 mg/100g each, plus pyruvic) were entirely missing from the FooDB profile and were added. Onion is defined by its organosulfur cysteine-sulfoxides (isoalliin), a real S/C source retained qualitatively. Low fat, no B12, low protein; 25 non-nutritive trace/contaminant elements and implausible animal/synthetic compounds removed.
